remember: Students will be able to recall the different methods of valuing goodwill on retirement of a partner.
Students will be able to identify the journal entries for adjusting the capital accounts on retirement of a partner.
Students will be able to distinguish between the different methods of settling the share of the deceased partner's in the assets and liabilities of the firm.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of reconstitution of partnership on retirement of a partner.
Students will be able to comprehend the steps involved in adjusting the capital accounts on retirement of a partner.
Students will be able to understand the concept of reconstitution of partnership on death of a partner.
apply: Students will be able to apply the methods of valuing goodwill on retirement of a partner.
Students will be able to prepare the journal entries for adjusting the capital accounts on retirement of a partner.
Students will be able to settle the share of the deceased partner's in the assets and liabilities of the firm.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of retirement of a partner on the capital structure of the firm.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of death of a partner on the capital structure of the firm.
Students will be able to compare the different methods of valuing goodwill on retirement of a partner.

ncert_objectives: To describe the methods of valuing goodwill on retirement of a partner.
To explain the steps involved in adjusting the capital accounts on retirement of a partner.
To discuss the methods of settling the share of the deceased partner's in the assets and liabilities of the firm.
board_exam_objectives: To solve problems related to retirement and death of a partner, including calculation of goodwill, adjustment of capital accounts, and settlement of the deceased partner's share.
To identify and apply the correct method of valuing goodwill on retirement of a partner.
To prepare journal entries for adjusting the capital accounts on retirement and death of a partner.
